meta.title: Privacy policy
meta.description: How Jobbliggaren processes your personal data: purposes, legal basis, retention periods, your rights and security. No tracking and no AI.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.intro: This privacy policy describes how Jobbliggaren processes your personal data, why, on what legal basis and for how long. It also covers your rights and how to exercise them. Jobbliggaren is a free service that helps you find and keep track of your job applications, built on open data about job openings from Arbetsförmedlingen JobTech (Platsbanken).

sections.heading: Controller and contact
paragraphs: The controller for the processing of your personal data in Jobbliggaren is Klas Olsson, a private individual, who runs Jobbliggaren as a non-profit hobby project. You can reach the controller at klasolsson81@gmail.com with any question about this policy and about how your personal data is processed.
It is the controller who determines why and how your data is processed, and who is responsible for ensuring that the processing complies with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

sections.heading: Data protection officer
paragraphs: Jobbliggaren has not appointed a data protection officer. The service is not subject to the requirement for a data protection officer under Article 37 of the General Data Protection Regulation. Instead, you contact us directly using the contact details above for any question about your personal data.

sections.heading: Account, profile and applications (legal basis: contract)
paragraphs: In order for you to create an account and use Jobbliggaren, we process the data below. The legal basis is that the processing is necessary for us to deliver the service to you (Article 6(1)(b), performance of a contract). Without this data, we cannot provide an account.
Your CV, your cover letters, your notes and your follow-ups are more sensitive data and are protected with encryption, see the section on security further down.
list: Email address, password (stored as a cryptographic hash, never in plaintext), display name and account identifier, as well as an identifier from an external login service if you log in via one. Used to create and secure your account and to identify you in the service.

Display name, language preference and your matching preferences, for example occupation, region, type of employment and skills you want to match against. Used to show relevant jobs and to tailor the service to your choices.
Your CV and content parsed from it. Used to store, display and work with your CV in the service.
Your applications, cover letters, personal notes and follow-ups linked to jobs. Used to help you keep track of your applications and to remind you about follow-ups.

sections.heading: Security, operations and product features (legal basis: legitimate interest)
paragraphs: For certain purposes we rely on legitimate interest (Article 6(1)(f)). This means that we have carried out a balancing test and concluded that the processing is necessary for our legitimate interest and does not override your interest in the protection of your data. You have the right to object to processing based on legitimate interest, see the section on your rights.
list: Security and event log. We log events in the service, for example logins, together with the IP address and browser information. Our legitimate interest is to protect the service and your account against misuse, intrusion and incorrect use, and to be able to investigate security events.

Retrieval of job ads from JobTech. We retrieve and process open data about job openings from Arbetsförmedlingen JobTech. Our legitimate interest is to be able to offer a functioning job service. The ads we retrieve are public data and are not your personal data.

Marking what is new since your last visit. We keep track of what is new for you since you were last logged in, so that you can quickly see what has been added. Our legitimate interest is to give you a useful and clear service.

sections.heading: Background watch notifications (legal basis: consent)
paragraphs: If you want, you can turn on notifications about new jobs that match your preferences. That processing only takes place if you actively turn it on yourself. The legal basis is your consent (Article 6(1)(a)). The feature is off by default. If you turn it on, we store a record that you gave your consent and when, so that we can demonstrate that the processing is lawful.

You can withdraw your consent at any time by turning off the notifications in your settings. We then stop sending them. Withdrawing your consent does not affect the fact that the processing was lawful before you withdrew it.

sections.heading: Recipients of data
paragraphs: Jobbliggaren never sells your data and does not share it for marketing purposes. In order to operate the service, we engage certain suppliers that process data on our behalf (data processors). We have data processing agreements with them.
Arbetsförmedlingen JobTech (Platsbanken) is our source of open data about job openings, not a recipient of your personal data. We do not send any data about you to JobTech.

Jobbliggaren currently does not send any email notifications, and no data about you is disclosed to any email provider. If email notifications are introduced in the future, we will update this policy and inform you about which provider is engaged and how your data is protected before the feature is activated.
list: Hetzner (planned hosting provider, EU). Jobbliggaren is planned to be hosted at Hetzner in the EU, where the application and the database run. This is planned and not yet in operation.
Cloudflare (planned DNS and CDN provider, EU). Cloudflare is planned to handle name resolution and traffic up to the service. This is planned and not yet in operation.

sections.heading: Transfer to third countries
paragraphs: We process your data within the EU/EEA. At present, no transfers of your personal data take place to countries outside the EU/EEA. If we were to engage a supplier outside the EU/EEA in the future, we will ensure a legal basis for the transfer under Chapter V of the General Data Protection Regulation, for example the European Commission's standard contractual clauses or a valid adequacy decision, and we will update this policy before any such transfer takes place.

sections.heading: How long we keep data
paragraphs: We do not keep your data longer than necessary for the purposes above.
list: Your account and content (CV, applications, letters, notes, follow-ups) are kept for as long as your account is active, so that you can use the service. You can delete individual content or your entire account at any time.

When you delete your account, the data is removed and the encrypted data is made permanently unreadable, see the section on security. After you have requested deletion, there is a 30-day period during which an account can be restored, before it is deleted permanently.
The security and event log is kept for 90 days and is then cleared automatically. When your account is deleted permanently, the data in the log that can be linked to you is de-identified.
Job ads from JobTech are cleared within 30 days.

sections.heading: Your rights
paragraphs: Under the General Data Protection Regulation, you have the following rights. You exercise them by contacting us at the email address above. We respond without undue delay and, as a rule, within one month.
list: Access. You have the right to find out whether we process data about you and to obtain a copy of it.
Rectification. You have the right to have inaccurate data corrected and incomplete data completed. Some data, such as profile and CV, you can also change directly in the service.
Erasure. You have the right to have your data erased. You can delete your account in the service, which removes your data.
Restriction. You have the right to request that the processing be restricted in certain cases, for example while an objection or a question about accuracy is being investigated.
Objection. You have the right to object to processing based on legitimate interest. We then stop that processing unless we have compelling legitimate grounds that override your interest.

Data portability. For data you have provided yourself and that is processed on the basis of a contract or consent, you have the right to receive it in a structured, commonly used and machine-readable format, and to have it transferred to another party where technically possible.

Withdraw consent. Where the processing is based on your consent, you can withdraw your consent at any time in your settings, without it affecting the lawfulness of the processing carried out beforehand.

sections.heading: Complaint to a supervisory authority
paragraphs: If you consider that we process your personal data incorrectly, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the Swedish Authority for Privacy Protection (IMY), which is the supervisory authority for data protection in Sweden. You can find contact details and information at imy.se. We are also happy to receive your feedback directly, so that we can correct any errors.

sections.heading: Requirements for using the service
paragraphs: To create an account you need to provide an email address. It is necessary for us to be able to identify you, secure your account and communicate with you about the service. You cannot create an account without an email address. Providing the data is a requirement for entering into the agreement to use Jobbliggaren, not a statutory requirement. Other data, such as CV and matching preferences, you provide yourself when and if you want to use those features.

sections.heading: No automated decisions and no profiling
paragraphs: Jobbliggaren makes no automated decisions and uses no profiling with legal or similarly significant effect for you. There is no such decision-making within the meaning of Article 22 of the General Data Protection Regulation.

The matching between you and jobs is rule-based, predictable and explainable. It is based on the preferences and data you provide yourself and on a fixed comparison against the ads. We always show which keywords match and which are missing, so that you can see why a job is suggested. Jobbliggaren uses no AI and no language models for matching, assessment or decisions. It is always you who decides which jobs you apply for.

sections.heading: Security
paragraphs: We protect your data with technical and organisational measures. Your most sensitive data, that is your CV, your cover letters, your notes and your follow-ups, is encrypted field by field with strong encryption (AES-256-GCM) and with an encryption key that is unique to you.

When you delete your account, your encryption key is destroyed. This makes your encrypted data permanently unreadable and impossible to recreate, even from a possible backup (so-called crypto-erasure). In this way your data truly disappears when you choose to leave the service.

sections.heading: Cookies
paragraphs: Jobbliggaren uses only necessary and functional first-party cookies and no tracking, analytics or marketing cookies. How cookies and browser storage work is described in our cookie policy.

sections.heading: Changes to this policy
paragraphs: We may update this privacy policy, for example if the service develops or if requirements change. The latest version is always available on this page, with the date of the most recent update at the top. For more significant changes, we will inform you in an appropriate way.
